points.text: Split the sequential API calls into parallel async tasks (e.g., asyncio.gather) to reduce overall latency.
Profile the SQL with EXPLAIN; identify missing indexes on join columns and add them.
Replace wide joins with sub‑queries or CTEs that filter early, limiting row sets before the final join.
Select only needed columns and add WHERE clauses to prune data; avoid SELECT * on large tables.
Introduce pagination or batch processing for large result sets to keep each query fast.
Set a per‑request timeout (e.g., 5 s) in the HTTP client and enforce a DB timeout via SET statement_timeout.
Implement retry with exponential backoff and circuit‑breaker to avoid cascading timeouts.
Split the monolithic query into smaller, focused queries or use CTEs to materialize intermediate results.
Ensure every join column has a matching index; add composite indexes for multi‑column filters.
Remove unnecessary tables/columns from the join; select only needed columns to reduce I/O.
Replace SELECT * with explicit column list and use covering indexes to avoid lookups.
Add WHERE predicates early to filter rows before joins; push predicates down with index hints if needed.
Analyze the execution plan; rewrite sub‑queries or use EXISTS/IN instead of costly LEFT JOINs.
Cache static lookup tables or denormalize frequently accessed reference data.
Set a reasonable client‑side timeout (e.g., 30 s) and enforce a server‑side statement_timeout.
Run the calls in parallel or async if they are independent, reducing overall latency.
